papers.title: Abscisic acid inhibits hypocotyl elongation acting on gibberellins, DELLA proteins and auxin

edges.mechanism: UV-B induces RUP1 transcription via UVR8/COP1/HY5 pathway
edges.evidence_sentence: RUP1 and RUP2 genes were transcriptionally activated by UV-B in a COP1-, UVR8-, and HY5-dependent manner

edges.mechanism: UV-B induces RUP2 transcription via UVR8/COP1/HY5 pathway
edges.evidence_sentence: Quantitative RT-PCR confirmed their early responsiveness to supplementary narrowband UV-B radiation

edges.mechanism: RUP1 directly binds UVR8 monomer and promotes UVR8 redimerization, halting UV-B signaling
edges.evidence_sentence: RUP proteins interact directly with UVR8 as potent repressors of UV-B signaling

edges.mechanism: RUP2 directly binds UVR8 to drive redimerization, terminating UV-B signaling
edges.evidence_sentence: Reconstitution of a functional YFP signal from complementary split YFP parts attached to UVR8 and RUP1 or RUP2 was clearly identified

edges.mechanism: HY5 directly activates RUP1 transcription as part of UV-B negative feedback loop
edges.evidence_sentence: RUP1/RUP2 transcriptional activation by UV-B is HY5-dependent

edges.mechanism: HY5 directly activates RUP2 transcription
edges.evidence_sentence: RUP1/RUP2 transcriptional activation requires HY5

edges.mechanism: RUP1 dampens UV-B-induced hypocotyl growth inhibition by inactivating UVR8; rup1 rup2 has shorter hypocotyl under UV-B
edges.evidence_sentence: rup1 rup2 double mutants showed a strong hypersensitivity to supplementary narrowband UV-B radiation at the level of hypocotyl growth inhibition

edges.mechanism: RUP2 indirectly promotes hypocotyl elongation by repressing UV-B signaling; RUP2-OX has long hypocotyl under UV-B
edges.evidence_sentence: RUP2 overexpression resulted in reduced UV-B-induced photomorphogenesis

edges.mechanism: GA4 binds GID1A to enable DELLA recruitment
edges.evidence_sentence: GA promotes interaction in yeast two-hybrid assays and GA-mediated degradation of RGA is abolished in the gid1 mutants

edges.mechanism: GA4 binds GID1B; GID1B has highest affinity for DELLA proteins
edges.evidence_sentence: GID1b pulled down RGA from sly1 extracts in a GA-dependent manner

edges.mechanism: GA4 binds GID1C to enable DELLA degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: GID1c pulled down RGA in GA-dependent manner, similar to GID1a and GID1b

edges.mechanism: GA-bound GID1A binds RGA and recruits SCF(SLY1) for ubiquitination/degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: GA-mediated degradation of RGA is abolished in the gid1 mutants

edges.mechanism: GID1B binds RGA with highest affinity, mediates DELLA degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: GID1b has higher affinity for DELLA protein than GID1a and GID1c

edges.mechanism: GID1C binds RGA in GA-dependent manner
edges.evidence_sentence: GID1c pulled down RGA in GA-dependent manner

edges.mechanism: GID1A binds GAI for ubiquitination and degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: DELLA domain mediates the interaction with GA INSENSITIVE DWARF1A receptor

edges.mechanism: GID1A is the primary GA receptor for stem/hypocotyl elongation; gid1a gid1c double has 76% reduced stem
edges.evidence_sentence: GID1a is the primary GA receptor stimulating stem elongation in proteolytic and non-proteolytic signaling

edges.mechanism: GID1B contributes redundantly to GA-promoted growth
edges.evidence_sentence: GID1B redundantly contributes to GA-induced stem elongation

edges.mechanism: GID1C cooperates with GID1A in stem elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: GID1c redundantly contributes to stem elongation

edges.mechanism: TCP5 directly activates PIF4 transcription, contributing to thermomorphogenesis
edges.evidence_sentence: TCP5, TCP13, TCP17 play fundamental roles in promoting thermoresponsive hypocotyl growth by positively regulating PIF4 activity

edges.mechanism: TCP13 redundantly activates PIF4
edges.evidence_sentence: Three TCP transcription factors play fundamental roles in promoting thermoresponsive growth by positively regulating PIF4

edges.mechanism: TCP17 physically interacts with PIF4 in temperature-dependent manner enhancing transcriptional activity
edges.evidence_sentence: TCP17 physically interacts with PIF4 in vivo via coIP assays; high temperature promotes the interaction

edges.mechanism: TCP17 associates with G-box regions in YUC8 promoter as PIF4 cofactor
edges.evidence_sentence: ChIP assays showed TCP17 associates with G-box-motif-containing regions in the promoters of YUC8 and IAA19

edges.mechanism: TCP17 cooperates with PIF4 at IAA19 promoter
edges.evidence_sentence: TCP17 associates with G-box regions in IAA19 promoter; association enhanced at 28C

edges.mechanism: CRY1 binds TCP17 at low temperature, sequestering TCP17 away from PIF4
edges.evidence_sentence: CRY1 physically interacts with TCP17 in a temperature-dependent manner; interaction greatly reduced at 28C but strong at 22C

edges.mechanism: TCP17 promotes thermomorphogenesis via PIF4 stabilization; TCP17-OX has constitutively long hypocotyl at 22C
edges.evidence_sentence: Transgenic seedlings overexpressing TCP17 showed greatly elongated hypocotyls at 22C

edges.mechanism: phyB modulates BAS1 expression in shoot apex; phyB-9 reduces BAS1 expression
edges.evidence_sentence: BAS1-GUS expression in shoot apex was much more prominent in wild-type PHYB background than phyB-9 background in red light

edges.mechanism: BAS1/CYP734A1 catalyzes C-26 hydroxylation inactivating BL and CS
edges.evidence_sentence: BAS1 hydroxylates brassinolide and castasterone to their respective inactive C-26 hydroxy products

edges.mechanism: SOB7/CYP72C1 inactivates BR biosynthesis precursors
edges.evidence_sentence: SOB7 acts on precursors of BR biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: BAS1 catabolism reduces BR levels; BAS1-OX suppresses long-hypocotyl phenotype of phyB-4
edges.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of BAS1/CYP734A1 suppresses long-hypocotyl phenotype of phyB-4

edges.mechanism: SOB7 catabolism reduces BR levels; SOB7-OX suppresses long-hypocotyl phenotype of phyB-4
edges.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of SOB7 suppresses long-hypocotyl phenotype of phyB-4

edges.mechanism: BBX11 directly binds HY5 promoter T/G-box and activates transcription
edges.evidence_sentence: BBX11 directly binds to the HY5 promoter region B containing E-box and T/G-box cis-elements to activate transcription

edges.mechanism: HY5 directly binds BBX11 promoter G-box and activates transcription, completing positive feedback
edges.evidence_sentence: HY5 associates with BBX11 promoter containing G-box cis-element to activate expression

edges.mechanism: BBX21 binds BBX11 promoter and positively regulates expression
edges.evidence_sentence: BBX21 binds BBX11 promoter and positively regulates its expression

edges.mechanism: BBX11 activates HY5 to inhibit hypocotyl elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: Loss-of-function bbx11 mutants display significantly elongated hypocotyls in red light and long day

edges.mechanism: phyA contributes to BBX11 expression in far-red light
edges.evidence_sentence: BBX11 expression significantly decreased in phyA-211 mutant under far-red light

edges.mechanism: phyB drives BBX11 expression in red light
edges.evidence_sentence: BBX11 expression significantly decreased in phyB-9 mutant under red light

edges.mechanism: CRY1 activates BBX11 expression in blue light
edges.evidence_sentence: BBX11 expression significantly decreased in cry1-304 mutant under blue light

edges.mechanism: DET1 stabilizes BBX11 protein
edges.evidence_sentence: YFP signals barely detectable in YFP-BBX11 det1-1; DET1 stabilizes BBX11 protein

edges.mechanism: PIF4 directly binds G-box in ATHB2 promoter to activate shade-induced expression
edges.evidence_sentence: ATHB2 induction by FR-enriched light is significantly diminished in pif4 pif5 and pifq mutants; ATHB2 is direct target of PIF proteins

edges.mechanism: PIF5 directly activates HFR1 transcription as part of shade-induced negative feedback
edges.evidence_sentence: HFR1/SICS1 is rapidly induced by FR-enriched light and recognized in vivo by PIF5

edges.mechanism: ATHB2 HD-Zip TF promotes shade-induced hypocotyl elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: Lack of ATHB2 function results in diminished hypocotyl elongation in low R/FR; elevated ATHB2 phenotype resembles shade

edges.mechanism: ATHB4 promotes shade-induced elongation; OE causes shade-like phenotype
edges.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of ATHB4 causes phenotypes analogous to shade response even in high R/FR

edges.mechanism: HAT1 (HD-Zip II) cooperates with ATHB2/ATHB4 in shade response
edges.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of HAT1 causes phenotypes analogous to plants with elevated ATHB2 in high R/FR

edges.mechanism: HAT2 promotes shade response
edges.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of HAT2 causes shade-like phenotypes in high R/FR

edges.mechanism: HAT3 cooperates with ATHB4 to promote shade-induced elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: In hat3 athb4 double loss-of-function mutant hypocotyl elongation is impaired

edges.mechanism: HD-Zip III REV directly activates ATHB2 transcription
edges.evidence_sentence: Direct regulation of ATHB2 by HD-Zip III transcription factors

edges.mechanism: PIF4 directly activates YUC2 in shade
edges.evidence_sentence: YUC2, YUC5, YUC8, and YUC9 are rapidly regulated by low R/FR ratio light through PIF transcription factors

edges.mechanism: PIF5 directly activates YUC5 in shade
edges.evidence_sentence: YUC genes directly link perception of shade light to plant growth through PIF-mediated induction

edges.mechanism: Light-activated phyA Pfr binds FHY1 enabling nuclear import
edges.evidence_sentence: FHY1 and FHL preferentially interact with the light-activated form of phyA

edges.mechanism: FHY1 acts as adapter mediating phyA nuclear import via NLS-importin pathway
edges.evidence_sentence: Nuclear accumulation of phyA is dependent on two related proteins called FHY1 and FHL

edges.mechanism: FHL functionally redundant with FHY1 in mediating phyA nuclear import
edges.evidence_sentence: FHL is required for full phytochrome A signaling and shares overlapping functions with FHY1

edges.mechanism: FHY3 transcription factor directly activates FHY1 expression
edges.evidence_sentence: FHY3 and FAR1 directly control FHY1 and FHL transcription

edges.mechanism: FAR1 redundantly activates FHY1 transcription with FHY3
edges.evidence_sentence: FHY3 and FAR1 directly control FHY1 and FHL transcription

edges.mechanism: FHY3 activates FHL transcription
edges.evidence_sentence: FHY3 and FAR1 directly control FHY1 and FHL transcription

edges.mechanism: FAR1 activates FHL transcription
edges.evidence_sentence: FHY3 and FAR1 directly control FHY1 and FHL transcription

edges.mechanism: FHY1 promotes hypocotyl inhibition by enabling phyA nuclear import; fhy1 has long hypocotyl in FR
edges.evidence_sentence: fhy1 mutants were strongly hyposensitive to FR light in FR-HIR for hypocotyl inhibition

edges.mechanism: FHL contributes to phyA-mediated hypocotyl inhibition
edges.evidence_sentence: fhy1-3/FHL RNAi lines display long hypocotyl phenotype

edges.mechanism: FHY3 activates FHY1 to promote phyA signaling and hypocotyl inhibition
edges.evidence_sentence: fhy3 mutants showed impaired responses to far-red light

edges.mechanism: FAR1 redundantly with FHY3 promotes phyA signaling
edges.evidence_sentence: FHY3/FAR1 are required for far-red induced hypocotyl inhibition

edges.mechanism: FHY1 binds LAF1 N-terminal R2R3 domain to assemble phyA signaling complex
edges.evidence_sentence: FHY1 and FHL interacted with full-length LAF1 and LAF1-N containing R2R3 domain

edges.mechanism: FHY1 N-terminus binds HFR1 C-terminus, scaffolding phyA-HFR1-LAF1 complex
edges.evidence_sentence: N-terminal regions of FHY1 and FHL interact with HFR1 in vitro

edges.mechanism: FHL binds LAF1 in parallel to FHY1
edges.evidence_sentence: FHL interacted with full-length LAF1 and LAF1-N

edges.mechanism: FHL binds HFR1 redundantly with FHY1
edges.evidence_sentence: Both FHY1 and FHL associated mostly with C-terminal fragment of HFR1

edges.mechanism: LAF1 MYB TF transmits phyA signal to inhibit hypocotyl elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: LAF1 independently transmits phyA signals for inhibition of hypocotyl elongation

edges.mechanism: BBX25 forms inactive heterodimer with HY5 via B-box-bZIP interaction
edges.evidence_sentence: BBX25 physically interacts with HY5 through B-box domains with bZIP domain of HY5, reducing HY5 transcriptional activity

edges.mechanism: BBX24 binds HY5 to inhibit transcriptional activity (same mechanism as BBX25)
edges.evidence_sentence: BBX24 and BBX25 repress BBX22 expression by interfering with HY5 transcriptional activity

edges.mechanism: HY5 binds BBX22 promoter and activates transcription
edges.evidence_sentence: HY5 binds to the BBX22 promoter and promotes its expression

edges.mechanism: COP1 ubiquitinates BBX25 for proteasomal degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: BBX25 undergoes COP1-mediated degradation via 26S Proteasome Pathway

edges.mechanism: BBX25 represses HY5 to promote hypocotyl elongation; bbx25 has shorter hypocotyl, BBX25-OX longer
edges.evidence_sentence: BBX25-overexpressing lines showed larger hypocotyls than Col-0 seedlings

edges.mechanism: BBX22 enhances HY5 function to inhibit hypocotyl growth
edges.evidence_sentence: BBX22 physically and genetically interacts with HY5 and enhances the function of HY5 by regulating hypocotyl growth

edges.mechanism: ABA represses GA3ox1 transcription, reducing GA biosynthesis
edges.evidence_sentence: ABA treatment significantly down-regulated GA biosynthetic genes; AtGA3ox1 expression was 12.5-fold lower

edges.mechanism: ABA represses GA20ox1 transcription
edges.evidence_sentence: AtGA20ox1 expression level 4.3-fold lower than mock-treated controls in red light

edges.mechanism: ABA induces GA2ox catabolic genes to reduce active GA
edges.evidence_sentence: GA catabolic gene AtGA2ox2 showed 2.1-fold increased expression in red light

edges.mechanism: ABA stabilizes DELLA proteins (RGA, GAI) by reducing GA-induced degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: ABA treatment significantly increased GAI and RGA protein level under red light

edges.mechanism: ABA represses YUC3 auxin biosynthesis gene
edges.evidence_sentence: YUC3 auxin biosynthetic gene was significantly down-regulated by ABA independently of light conditions

edges.mechanism: ABA inhibits hypocotyl elongation via reduced GA, stabilized DELLA, repressed PIF, reduced auxin
edges.evidence_sentence: Wild-type seedlings grown under red light or in dark were significantly shorter when treated with increasing concentrations of ABA

perturbations.evidence_sentence: rup1 rup2 double mutants showed strong hypersensitivity to UV-B at level of hypocotyl growth inhibition
RUP2 overexpression resulted in reduced UV-B-induced photomorphogenesis
rup2 single mutant already showed weak UV-B hypersensitivity
Semidwarf phenotype with stem elongation reduced by 76% compared to wildtype
Double mutant displayed reduced fertility but no severe hypocotyl defects, 14% taller
Double mutant displayed essentially wild-type phenotype
3tcp triple mutant showed more significant thermoresponsive defect than double mutant
35S::TCP17-FLAG transgenic seedlings showed greatly elongated hypocotyls at 22C
Transgenic seedlings overexpressing TCP5 showed greatly elongated hypocotyls at 22C
Transgenic seedlings overexpressing TCP13 showed greatly elongated hypocotyls at 22C
tcp17 single mutant showed hypocotyl response similar to Col-0
Overexpression of BAS1 suppresses long-hypocotyl phenotype of phyB-4; null mutant shows opposite effect
Overexpression of SOB7 suppresses long-hypocotyl phenotype of phyB-4
bas1-2 sob7-1 double mutant confers BR-deficient phenotype typified by det2-1
Overexpression of BAS1/CYP734A1 suppresses long-hypocotyl phenotype of phyB-4
Two independent bbx11 loss-of-function mutants displayed significantly elongated hypocotyls
BBX11 overexpression seedlings showed markedly shortened hypocotyls when grown in various light conditions
bbx11-1 bbx21-1 double mutant showed additive phenotype with significantly longer hypocotyls
Lack of ATHB2 function results in diminished hypocotyl elongation in low R/FR
Seedlings with elevated ATHB2 levels in high R/FR resemble wild-type phenotype in shade
Overexpression of ATHB4 causes phenotypes analogous to shade response even in high R/FR
In hat3 athb4 double loss-of-function mutant hypocotyl elongation is impaired
Triple pin3 pin4 pin7 mutant does not elongate under simulated shade
fhy1 mutants were strongly hyposensitive to FR light in FR-HIR for hypocotyl inhibition
fhy1-3/FHL RNAi lines display long hypocotyl phenotype
fhy3 mutants showed impaired responses to far-red light
far1 mutants are hyposensitive to far-red light
hfr1-201 mutants hyposensitive to far-red continuous light; hypocotyl lengths shorter than fhy1-3
LAF1 Ri lines show hyposensitivity to far-red light
bbx25 seedlings displayed significantly shorter hypocotyls than Col-0 wild-type in white light, red, FR, blue light
BBX25-overexpressing lines showed larger hypocotyls than Col-0 seedlings
bbx25-2 bbx24-1 double mutant significantly shorter than each single mutant
Wild-type seedlings under red light or dark were significantly shorter when treated with increasing concentrations of ABA
pifq mutant has constitutive photomorphogenic phenotype with insensitivity to ABA
phototropins (PHOT1, PHOT2) primarily mediate phototropism; minor role in hypocotyl elongation under blue light
MG132 proteasome inhibitor treatment markedly increased EIL1 protein levels and stabilizes EBF1/2 and EIN3
AVG ethylene biosynthesis inhibitor reduces ethylene-induced growth inhibition
